Georgia Audiologist License

Requirements, fees, and steps for Georgia audiologist license.

Last updated February 25, 2026 · Sources verified

At a glance

Application fee
$100
Required training
State-approved program
Required exam
Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series offered by the Educational Testing Service
Estimated processing time
4 to 6 Weeks
Licensing board
Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

4 to 6 Weeks

Required
Temporary license
Available
Path

Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years

$75
CE required
20 hours every 2 years
Licensing board
Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
Renew on board site (opens in new tab)

Last updated February 25, 2026.

Become a Audiologist in Georgia

Applicants for Audiologist licensure in Georgia must apply online through the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS).

What You'll Need

  • Official Transcripts — Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report — Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.
  • Fingerprint background check — through IdentoGO / GCIC
  • Application fee — $100

How to Apply

  1. Step 1

    Create an Account and Apply Online

    Register for an account on the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS) and complete the online application for Audiologist licensure, indicating if you are applying by examination or endorsement.

  2. Step 2

    Submit Supporting Documents

    Upload all required documents, including official transcripts verifying a doctorate degree from a CAA-accredited program, proof of U.S. citizenship or legal alien status, and any other forms specified in the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS) application.

  3. Step 3

    Arrange for Official Scores

    Request that official scores for the National Examination in Audiology (Praxis Exam) be sent directly to the Georgia State Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology from the testing agency.

  4. Step 4

    Pay Application Fees

    Remit all required application fees, including the initial application fee, through the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS).

  5. Step 5

    Respond to Board Requests

    Monitor your application status within the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS) and be prepared to provide any additional information or documentation requested by the Board during the review process.

What Usually Slows Applicants Down

  • Fingerprinting delays — Out-of-state prints can add 2-4 weeks to your timeline.
  • Missing or incomplete documents — Have all your documents ready before starting the application to avoid processing delays.
  • Application errors — Double-check that your name matches across all documents exactly. Mismatches are a common reason for delays.

Out-of-State Audiologists

Georgia is an active member of the ASLP-IC. If your home state also participates and your license is eligible, you can obtain a compact privilege to practice in Georgia.

If you do not qualify for the compact, you can apply for licensure using the traditional endorsement pathway detailed below.

Key Timeline Factors

Keep these requirements in mind, as they are the most likely to impact your processing timeline in Georgia.

  • Fingerprinting: Required. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Georgia can add 2-4 weeks.
  • License verification: Georgia requires verification of your current out-of-state license before your file is complete.
  • Compact status: ASLP-IC is active in Georgia.
  • Temporary license: The board lists a temporary or provisional route while the full file is pending.

Georgia Endorsement Requirements

This is the step-by-step endorsement checklist for already-licensed audiologists applying to practice in Georgia.

  1. Step 1

    Submit Your Application

    Apply through the Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology. The application fee is $100.

  2. Step 2

    Provide License Verification

    Georgia requires official verification of your active out-of-state license. This must be sent directly to the board from your current state board or its official online verification system before your file can be considered complete.

  3. Step 3

    Request Official Transcripts

    Sent directly from your educational institution.

  4. Step 4

    Submit Your Exam Score Report

    Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

  5. Step 5

    Complete Fingerprinting and Background Screening

    Georgia requires a criminal background check for all applicants. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Georgia can add 2-4 weeks to your processing time.

  6. Step 6

    Await Board Processing

    Plan for a processing timeline of about 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete. If you need to start working immediately, the board offers a temporary or provisional licensing route that you can utilize while your full application is pending.

  7. Step 7

    Create an Account and Apply Online

    Register for an account on the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS) and complete the online application for Audiologist licensure, indicating if you are applying by examination or endorsement.

  8. Step 8

    Arrange for Official Scores

    Request that official scores for the National Examination in Audiology (Praxis Exam) be sent directly to the Georgia State Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology from the testing agency.

  9. Step 9

    Respond to Board Requests

    Monitor your application status within the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS) and be prepared to provide any additional information or documentation requested by the Board during the review process.

Fees, Processing Time, and Renewal

Here is a complete breakdown of the costs and timelines associated with getting and maintaining your Georgia Audiologist license.

Initial Licensing Costs

Application fee
$100
Background check
$0 (included in application) (The board's processing fee is included in the application, but out-of-state fingerprinting services charge a separate fee of ~$40)
Total estimated cost
$100 (Covers your application fee plus the initial license issuance fee)

Processing Timeline

Estimated time
4 to 6 Weeks (once your application and fingerprinting are fully received by the board)

Temporary License

Georgia offers a temporary or provisional license so you can start working while your full endorsement application is processing.

Georgia Audiologist License Renewal

Maintain your active license and stay compliant with the Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

Ongoing License Renewal

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$75

Board Contact

Board
Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

Phone
404-424-9966

Sources verified February 25, 2026. Links go directly to official Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology pages.

Georgia Audiologist Licensing FAQ

How much does a Georgia audiologist license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Georgia have reciprocity for audiologists?

Yes. Georgia participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Georgia without a separate endorsement application.

How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Georgia?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

Trending States

  • Alabama
  • Florida
  • North Carolina
  • South Carolina

Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
